skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Generate AI images with FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve and 50+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: FLUX Dev LoRA, FLUX.2 Klein LoRA, Gemini 3 Pro Image, Grok Imagine, Seedream 4.5, Reve, ImagineArt. Capabilities: text-to-image, image-to-image, inpainting, LoRA, image editing, upscaling, text rendering. Use for: AI art, product mockups, concept art, social media graphics, marketing visuals, illustrations. Triggers: flux, image generation, ai image, text to image, stable diffusion, generate image, ai art, midjourney alternative, dall-e alternative, text2img, t2i, image generator, ai picture, create image with ai, generative ai, ai illustration, grok image, gemini image
skill.summary: Generate AI images with FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve and 50+ models via the belt CLI
skill.author: infsh-skills

security_audit.summary: Reviewed 1 file (148 lines). Static findings were evaluated: backtick execution (38) and hardcoded URLs (9) are false positives. The backtick detection was triggered by markdown code formatting in example commands. Hardcoded URLs are legitimate inference.sh service endpoints. No prompt injection or malicious patterns detected. Skill makes legitimate CLI calls to belt tool for AI image generation.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Service URLs
low_findings.description: The skill references inference.sh URLs and external AI service endpoints. These are expected for an image generation CLI skill and do not indicate exfiltration risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Hardcoded URLs are intentional for a CLI skill that connects to inference.sh services. No evidence of data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: CLI Command Documentation
low_findings.description: The skill documents belt CLI commands. Static analyzer flagged 'backtick execution' patterns in code blocks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backtick detection was triggered by markdown code formatting (inline code syntax), not actual shell command injection. Commands like 'belt login' and 'belt app run' are legitimate CLI invocations with hardcoded arguments.

content.value_statement: Creating AI-generated images requires working with multiple model APIs and complex parameters. This skill provides a unified CLI interface to FLUX, Gemini, Grok, and other image generation models through the belt tool.
actual_capabilities: Generate images using 50+ AI models including FLUX, Gemini 3 Pro, Grok Imagine, and Seedream
Create text-to-image artwork with various aspect ratios and quality settings
Upscale and enhance existing images with Topaz professional upscaler
Edit images with natural language prompts using Reve model
Browse and explore available image generation models via CLI
Generate images with multiple LoRA style presets
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI (belt) installation and authentication
Image quality and style depend on the underlying AI model capabilities
Generation time varies by model complexity and server load

use_cases.title: Create Marketing Visuals
use_cases.description: Generate product mockups, promotional images, and social media graphics using FLUX and P-Image models for marketing campaigns.

use_cases.title: Concept Art and Illustration
use_cases.description: Produce concept art, character designs, and illustrations for creative projects using Seedream 4.5 for cinematic 4K quality.

use_cases.title: Rapid Prototyping
use_cases.description: Quickly generate multiple image variations to explore design directions for products, UI mockups, and creative briefs.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Image
prompt_templates.prompt:
Use belt to generate an image with FLUX model. Create a {description} using:
belt app run falai/flux-dev-lora --input '{"prompt": "{description}"}'
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a simple image generated from a text description

prompt_templates.title: Specific Style and Aspect
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need an image with specific dimensions and artistic style

prompt_templates.title: Google Gemini Model
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to use Google AI for image generation

prompt_templates.title: High-Quality Cinematic
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need professional-quality cinematic images

output_examples.input: Generate a professional product photo of a leather briefcase
output_examples.output: An AI-generated image of a leather briefcase with studio lighting, displayed in your terminal or saved to file

output_examples.input: Create a cyberpunk cityscape at sunset with 16:9 aspect ratio
output_examples.output: A wide-format cyberpunk scene with neon lighting, generated via Grok Imagine model

best_practices: Start with clear, descriptive prompts that specify style, lighting, and composition
Use aspect_ratio parameter to match your intended output format (16:9 for social, 1:1 for posts)
Browse available models with 'belt app list --category image' to find the best model for your use case
anti_patterns: Do not concatenate user input directly into shell commands without escaping
Avoid vague prompts like 'make it look good' - be specific about style and subject
Do not assume unlimited API calls - check your inference.sh account usage

faq.question: How do I install the belt CLI?
faq.answer: Follow the install instructions at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/inference-sh/skills/refs/heads/main/cli-install.md and run 'belt login' to authenticate.

faq.question: Which model should I use for text with rendering?
faq.answer: Use Seedream 3.0 or Reve models. These are specifically designed for accurate text rendering in generated images.

faq.question: How do I generate images with specific dimensions?
faq.answer: Add the aspect_ratio parameter to your prompt, for example: --input '{"prompt": "...", "aspect_ratio": "16:9"}'

faq.question: Can I upscale existing images?
faq.answer: Yes, use the Topaz upscaler: 'belt app run falai/topaz-image-upscaler --input '{"image_url": "https://..."}''

faq.question: Which model is fastest?
faq.answer: FLUX Klein 4B (pruna/flux-klein-4b) is ultra-cheap at $0.0001 per image. P-Image models also offer fast generation.

faq.question: How do I browse all available image models?
faq.answer: Run 'belt app list --category image' to see all available image generation apps and models.
